metadata.title: The Demo, the Before/After, and Shipping the Files
metadata.playlist: Make It Smart — Scale Your Madison Intelligence
metadata.source: INFO 7375 Assignment 4 brief — Scale Your Thing & Add Intelligence

beats.narration_text: Hola — this is Liam. Episode 7 of 7. Three things close the assignment: the demo, the before and after, and four submission files with exact names. The demo proves the workflow runs. The before and after shows growth since Assignment 3. The deduction list is the fastest sanity check — run through it before you submit and you will not lose twenty-five points on a JSON output you forgot to convert. Let's close it out.

props.segment: The Demo, the Before/After, and Shipping the Files
props.command: Record the demo. Show the before/after. Name the 4 files correctly. Run the deduction checklist.
props.runningText: deduction list before submit — catch the -25 pts before the grader does…
output: Demo: 3–5 min walkthrough + backup plan if live demo fails
Before/after: A3 workflow vs A4 workflow — show the diff
4 files: workflow_v2.json · demo PDF/video · scale_test_results.md · outputs/

beats.act: the demo — structure and backup plan
beats.narration_text: The demo is three to five minutes. It has three parts. First: the walkthrough — show the Figma board, identify the AI capability, show the workflow in n8n. Second: run it live — click Execute on camera, show the records processing, show one output being produced. Third: show the output artifact — open the HTML report, the CSV, or whatever you built in Episode 3. Open it. Let the viewer read at least a few lines. Those three parts prove the workflow exists, runs, and produces something usable. Now the backup plan. The live demo will sometimes fail. The AI API may be slow. n8n may restart. The data file may not be where you expect it. A backup plan is a recording of the workflow running successfully that you made before the demo day. If anything fails live, you say 'here is a recording from earlier this week' and play it. One recording, unedited, same content as the live demo. You make the backup recording the day before the real one. If you do not have a backup recording and the live demo fails, you have no demo.

artifactLines: Part 1 — Walkthrough: Figma board → AI capability → n8n workflow.
Part 2 — Run it: Execute on camera → records processing → one output produced.
Part 3 — Show the artifact: open the HTML/CSV/output and let viewer read it.
Backup plan: record the workflow running successfully the day before the real demo.
If live demo fails: 'here is a recording from earlier this week' → play it.
props.sparkLine: Walkthrough. Run it. Show the artifact. Backup recording.

beats.act: the before/after and the deduction checklist
beats.narration_text: The before and after slide shows one record from Assignment 3 next to the same record after Assignment 4. A3: title, date, source, content. A4: title, date, source, content, plus all the AI-generated fields. That diff is the proof of growth. Put it on the Figma board or include it in the demo walkthrough. Now run the deduction checklist on camera or in your head before submitting. The rubric's deduction list: minus twenty points for no meaningful intelligence gain over Assignment 3 — check the before and after, confirm the diff shows real new fields. Minus twenty-five points for JSON-only output — open your output and verify a non-technical person can read it. Minus twenty points for fake scale testing results — open your scale_test_results.md and confirm every number comes from an actual test run. Minus fifteen points for missing or incomplete Figma board — open the Figma link and confirm both pages are there and both have real content. Minus ten points for no error handling — confirm the On-Error branch exists in the workflow and fires on a bad input.

artifactLines: Before/after: same record — A3 fields left, A4 fields (+ AI output) right. Show the diff.
Deduction checklist:
-20: no intelligence gain → check before/after shows new AI-generated fields.
-25: JSON-only output → open output file, confirm non-technical person can read it.
-20: fake scale test → open scale_test_results.md, confirm numbers from real runs.
-15: missing Figma → open link, confirm both pages with real content.
-10: no error handling → confirm On-Error branch exists and fires on bad input.
props.sparkLine: Run this checklist. Catch the deductions before the grader does.

beats.narration_text: The Canvas submission for Assignment 4 is four items. One: the workflow JSON file, named workflow_v2.json. Open it before submitting, search for your API key, confirm it is not there. Two: the demo walkthrough — either a video file or a PDF of screenshots, depending on your format. Name it demo_walkthrough.pdf or keep the video's original extension. Three: the scale test report, named scale_test_results.md. If you wrote it in another format, export it or copy it to a Markdown file with that name. Four: the outputs folder — a folder containing the actual output files from your gallery runs. The folder should have at least ten files — the HTML reports, CSV rows, or chart images from the gallery entries in Episode 4. Zip the folder if Canvas requires it. Link all four from the Figma board so the grader can navigate to every piece of evidence from one place. A submission where the grader has to hunt for the scale test results or the output folder is a submission that is harder to grade — and hard to grade tends to score lower.

artifactLines: 1. workflow_v2.json — search file for API key before submitting; must not appear.
2. demo_walkthrough.pdf (or video) — the 3–5 min demo.
3. scale_test_results.md — real numbers from actual test runs.
4. outputs/ folder — at least 10 output files from the gallery.
Link all four from the Figma board — grader navigates from one place.
props.sparkLine: 4 items. Named correctly. No API keys. Linked from Figma.

beats.narration_text: Verdict for The Demo and Submit. Demo: three to five minutes — walkthrough, run it live, show the artifact. Make the backup recording the day before. Before and after: same record, A3 fields left, A4 fields plus AI output right. Run the deduction checklist before submitting: no intelligence gain, JSON-only output, fake scale test, missing Figma, no error handling — five checks, five deductions avoided. Four Canvas files: workflow_v2.json, demo walkthrough, scale_test_results.md, and the outputs folder. All four linked from the Figma board. workflow_v2.json checked for API keys. That is the whole assignment. The Madison track continues in Assignment 5.

props.artifactTitle: The Demo, the Before/After, and Shipping the Files
artifactLines: Demo: 3–5 min. Walkthrough → run live → show artifact. Backup recording the day before.
Before/after: A3 fields left, A4 fields right. Show the diff.
Deduction checklist: -20 no intelligence · -25 JSON-only · -20 fake scale · -15 no Figma · -10 no errors.
4 files: workflow_v2.json · demo · scale_test_results.md · outputs/ folder.
Link all 4 from Figma. Check workflow JSON for API keys.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Before you record the real demo, do a dry run and use this prompt. Paste this into Claude — fill in the brackets with your specific workflow. The prompt reads: I am about to record a three-to-five minute demo of my n8n workflow for Assignment 4. The workflow does this: [one sentence on the AI capability]. It produces this output: [one sentence on the output format]. My three data sources are: [list them]. What are the five things most likely to fail during a live demo of this specific workflow — credential errors, slow AI responses, missing file paths, rate limits hitting at an unexpected point, or anything else specific to my setup — and how do I verify each one is ready before I click Record? Also flag anything in this workflow that, if it fails on camera, would be hard to recover from in under thirty seconds. That prompt is your pre-flight. Run through every item. Fix anything flagged. Record the backup first. Then record the real demo. A stumble in the dry run is a stumble you fixed before it mattered.
